Empires & Puzzles Costumed Sun Quan 5-Star Legendary Fire Red from Kingdom Family Review

Sun Quan's costume transforms him from a forgotten general into a terrifying Warlord. With the coveted 'Unstoppable Minions' passive and a massive January 2025 buff to his minion stats, he now commands an army that simply refuses to die. Is he the new king of burn?
Costumed Sun Quan: Unstoppable Fire & Fury

The "Warlord of Fortune" has arrived to reclaim his throne. Costumed Sun Quan (C1) takes everything the base hero tried to do and actually makes it work in the modern meta. He swaps his Ranger bow for Rogue daggers and brings the War of the Three Kingdoms' most annoying mechanic:
Released significantly later than the base version and receiving a
, this hero is a serious contender. He hits three enemies hard, summons beefy minions for everyone, and sets the battlefield ablaze with high-damage burn.

Gone is the slightly "goofy" stance of the original. The costume features Sun Quan in imposing, darker armor with jagged, flame-like motifs. He looks more aggressive, more battle-hardened, and ready to scorch the earth. The "Fire Bat" aesthetic (hinted at in the internal ID) really shines through with a sharper, more dynamic look.

Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities
The Kingdom bonus gives Mana and Health on minion summon. With his Average speed, he fuels the rest of your Kingdom heroes, creating a perpetual motion machine of minions and mana.
This is why you want him.
Grimble? Useless. Topaz? Useless. Eloise? Useless.
Bera and Kvasir cannot stop him from summoning.
way to get rid of these minions is to damage them until they die. This guarantees his team gets the protection and the burn application.

(since minion stats are based on the caster's current attack).

The January 2025 buff (increasing minion HP from 15% to 22% and Burn from 441 to 543) was critical. Before that, he was "good but soft." Now, he is a meta-relevant threat. He stands tall against modern heroes because his minions offer guaranteed value that cannot be countered by standard dispel/removal mechanics.
He is a fantastic flank or offensive support hero. The Unstoppable mechanic ensures he always provides value, regardless of the enemy's counter-picks.
It's not even a contest. The Costume provides:
(Dodge) vs Ranger (Bypass). The Dodge adds survivability, which is excellent for a minion summoner.
Attack/Defense/Health/Mana bonuses make him statistically relevant. The 5% Mana bonus is particularly juicy for breakpoints.
Max HP. The higher his health, the beefier the Unstoppable Minions. With 22% inheritance, every point of HP you give Sun Quan is huge value for the whole team.
Increases special skill damage and Minion Attack/Burn.
. The value of Unstoppable Minions lies in them staying alive to absorb damage and apply burn repeatedly. Make them as thick as possible.
Unstoppable Minions force the enemy to chew through HP; they can't just press a button to remove them.
The burn damage is significant and re-applies constantly.
He isn't "Rush" fast, though the mana bonus helps.
If the enemy has raw damage output (e.g., heavy AoE hitters), they can clear the minions through brute force.
Heroes immune to burn (e.g., Untold Tales 2 passives, Grazul) negate half his kit.
Unstoppable minions are a nightmare to deal with in timed battles.
He is S-Tier here. Minions provide "healing" and apply DoT.
Excellent against teams that rely on minion removal (Grimble users will cry).
Sun Quan C + Diaochan C + Liu Bei C.
All three kingdoms heroes with Unstoppable minions. The enemy simply cannot clear the board of meat shields.
Overwhelm the enemy with various DoTs (Burn, Poison, Sand) while hiding behind minions.

High damage AoE heroes (like Relius or Sha Wujing) don't care if minions are "unstoppable"; they just kill them with damage.
This is a jackpot pull. Prioritize leveling the Costume immediately. The stats alone will carry you, and the minions will save your team in difficult quests.

The January 2025 buff moved him from "meh" to "must-level." The increase to 22% Minion HP is a breakpoint that allows his minions to survive a standard slash attack. If you have Alpha Aethers, he is a worthy candidate for the
power to maximize his offensive pressure.
"Can’t believe this guy’s minions are that weak in HP... Should have been made fast like a fire version of Bera."
"Thoughts on the buff? ... Wu Soldier Minion Inherited HP increased: 15% -> 22%."
"It’s personal preference, but with the buff I think Sun Quan’s costume edges him out - I’d use him more often."
There is still debate about his match-up against newer heroes: "Regardless of the buff, he has a bad matchup against untold tales 2 heroes who are not only immune to burn, they heal from it."

Costumed Sun Quan is the redemption arc we wanted. He went from being the "weakest" Kingdom costume to a powerhouse of sustainability and damage. The combination of Unstoppable Minions with significantly buffed stats makes him a defensive stalwart and an offensive nuisance. He might not be the flashiest hero, but he wins wars of attrition.
A modern staple for Red decks and Minion teams.
The Warlord gets a massive upgrade! We review Costumed Sun Quan (C1), covering his Unstoppable Minions, improved burn damage, and how the Jan 2025 balance update makes him a top-tier threat.
